query: Prospective approaches to realising high-performance, sustainable VRFB membranes. is coupled with a mechanism-stress int...[truncated] If Prospective approaches to realising high-performance, sustainable VRFB membranes. is coupled with a mechanism-stress intervention that varies the key controllable variable named by the source evidence in electrochemical energy storage, then capacity decay | vanadium ions crossover | charge-discharge performance will expose a directional or non-monotonic boundary at a preregistered stress threshold rather than an open-ended improvement claim:  This proposal addresses the PaperGraph gap ''. It hypothesizes a testable mechanism: Concrete mechanism chain: (1) method capability: Prospective approaches to realising high-performance, sustainable VRFB membranes. contributes through a specified operation that must be mapped to observable inputs and outputs before validation; (2) scenario target: in electrochemical energy storage, the affected process is a measurable material, molecular, or reaction mechanism under controlled conditions; (3) measurable consequence: the bridge must produce a preregistered change in capacity decay | vanadium ions crossover | charge-discharge performance. The method's input requirements are broad or not clearly specified; the experiment must make them explicit. No explicit bridge concept is currently visible; this must be treated as a human-review assumption rather than a validated mechanism. The decisive prediction is that this concrete bridge, not a generic representation change, will alter capacity decay | vanadium ions crossover | charge-discharge performance; if the bridge data or causal link is absent, the hypothesis should fail rather than be reinterpreted post hoc. MingLi treats this as a structural mutation rather than a surface rephrasing. The study is designed to be falsifiable through Build a minimal benchmark for electrochemical energy storage; compare Prospective approaches to realising high-performance, sustainable VRFB membranes. against canonical baselines; measure capacity decay | vanadium ions crossover | charge-discharge performance; include negative controls, ablations, and failure-mode analysis. Grounding evidence comes from: Meng-Yue Lu et al. (2019) Mitigating capacity decay and improving charge-discharge performance of a vanadium redox flow battery with asymmetric operating conditions doi:10.1016/J.ELECTACTA.2019.04.032, Lingzhi Ye et al. (2024) Vanadium Redox Flow Battery: Review and Perspective of 3D Electrodes. doi:10.1021/acsnano.4c06675. The proposal differs by testing the mapped gap directly with explicit baselines, ablations, and failure-mode criteria.
